summary: Iterations 52 + 53 backend validation. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iter53_personal_docs_salary_kyc.py (19 tests, ALL PASS) and ran existing /app/backend/tests/test_iter52_textile_backdate_grouping.py (20/21 pass — 1 real bug). Regression: 47/47 iter49+50+51 tests still PASS. Two backend gaps flagged: (1) /api/attendance/policy/presets does not expose the 'textile' sub-category preset — the ATTENDANCE_POLICY_PRESETS dict contains a fully-formed 5-shift textile preset (server.py:715), but the endpoint (server.py:3597) only iterates BUSINESS_CATEGORIES top-level keys, and 'textile' is a sub-category of 'industry'. Consequence: UI can't fetch the 5 textile shifts as a preset; the same routing gap means _policy_for_category('industry') returns the 3-shift factory preset even for companies whose business_subcategory='Textile'. (2) /me/documents responses omit the 'uploaded_via' field — _emp_doc_public (server.py:8887) doesn't include it, so admins/UI cannot distinguish self-uploaded scans from HR-uploaded ones despite the field being persisted in Mongo. Everything else on iter52+53 spec — policy PATCH validation, textile compute-day (both variants), manual punch CRUD + audit log + 90-day guard + cross-company 403, employee grouping (type title-casing, is_onroll, unset filter, /employee-types), salary_mode (daily/hourly + 422 for 'yearly'), KYC lock (first-time set, change → 400 locked, name-only edit ok, PAN same rules) — all pass.

critical.issue: Response lacks a 'textile' preset entry. Endpoint iterates BUSINESS_CATEGORIES (server.py:3607) which only contains top-level keys (hospital, industry, ...) — 'textile' lives in ATTENDANCE_POLICY_PRESETS dict as a sub-category with the required 5 shifts + policy_variant='policy_1' + duty_hours_rounding_minutes=15 (server.py:715). Fix: append an explicit textile preset entry to the presets list (business_category='industry' + business_subcategory='Textile' or business_category='textile'), OR change _policy_for_category to fall back to the textile preset when business_subcategory=='Textile'. Iter52 test test_presets_include_textile FAILS on this.

minor.issue: _emp_doc_public serializer (server.py:8887) omits 'uploaded_via'. The POST handler stores uploaded_via='employee' (server.py:9288) but neither the create response nor GET listing returns it, so HR/UI cannot tell self-uploads from admin-uploads. Add 'uploaded_via': d.get('uploaded_via') to _emp_doc_public().

action_items: Add a textile preset entry to GET /api/attendance/policy/presets response (either as its own row or as an industry sub-category) so the Attendance Policy UI can offer it as a picker choice — server.py:3597.

Route business_subcategory='Textile' to the textile preset inside _policy_for_category (server.py:743) so a freshly created textile company auto-inherits the 5-shift + policy_variant='policy_1' + duty_hours_rounding_minutes=15 defaults on /attendance/policy GET.
Include 'uploaded_via' in _emp_doc_public() (server.py:8887) so /me/documents (GET/POST) and /admin/employees/{uid}/documents responses carry the self-upload marker.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1671-1673 — After stripping locked KYC keys (same-value no-op) update_own_kyc raises 400 'Nothing to update' if only aadhar/pan (with same value) was submitted. Spec says 'Same value → no-op OK'. Currently harmless because clients always send name_as_per_aadhar alongside, but consider returning 200 with unchanged kyc rather than 400 in the pure same-value case.
server.py:8887 _emp_doc_public — missing 'uploaded_via' field (bug flagged above). Also missing 'uploaded_by' name/email lookup, but that's cosmetic.

server.py:3597 list_attendance_policy_presets — loops only over BUSINESS_CATEGORIES; the textile preset is orphaned inside ATTENDANCE_POLICY_PRESETS dict. Recommend either (a) surfacing it as an explicit extra entry or (b) making BUSINESS_CATEGORIES rows carry a `preset_key` override so 'industry' can point at the textile preset when the sub-category selection warrants it.
seed_data_creation: Each new test creates its own throwaway company (TEST_Iter52_* / TEST_Iter53_*) with fresh employees via /companies + /employee-signup + /admin/approve-employee. Fixtures DELETE the throwaway companies on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter53 test file follows the same throwaway-company/OTP-login pattern as iter49/50/51/52. When testing /me/documents pay attention to _emp_doc_public — currently it omits `uploaded_via`. If the main agent adds that field, uncomment/re-enable the assertion at test_iter53_personal_docs_salary_kyc.py::test_upload_valid_doc_tagged_employee. For the textile preset bug, the fix will likely be one of: (a) add a subcategory-aware branch in list_attendance_policy_presets or (b) map 'industry' preset key to 'textile' when business_subcategory=='Textile' inside _policy_for_category. After the fix, the failing iter52 test test_presets_include_textile should turn green with no test changes.
rca_of_issue: (1) TEXTILE PRESET GAP — The ATTENDANCE_POLICY_PRESETS dict has a full 'textile' preset with the exact 5 shifts (Day 7-7, Day 8-8, Night 7-7, Night 8-8, General 9-5), policy_variant='policy_1', duty_hours_rounding_minutes=15 (server.py:715-737). But GET /attendance/policy/presets iterates only BUSINESS_CATEGORIES (server.py:3607-3613) which lists 'industry' at the top level with 'Textile' as a string sub-category — never as its own preset. Same for _policy_for_category(business_category) which only knows about top-level keys — so a company with business_category='industry' + business_subcategory='Textile' auto-inherits the 3-shift factory preset, not the intended textile preset. Repro: POST /api/companies with business_subcategory='Textile' then GET /api/attendance/policy?company_id=... → policy.shifts has 3 rows (Shift A/B/C 8h each), not the textile 5 rows. Fix scope: small. (2) uploaded_via HIDDEN — _emp_doc_public dict-construction (server.py:8891-8905) doesn't copy the 'uploaded_via' field even though POST /me/documents writes it. One-line fix.
